hypothesis:
Given that task-tool alignment (measured via embedding similarity) is critical for agent performance, yet current foundation models still lack sufficient domain knowledge in specialized scientific fields, I hypothesize:

**Dynamically fine-tuning tool embeddings during agent execution using retrieval-augmented generation from domain-specific corpora will reduce the semantic distance between tools and tasks more effectively than static tool descriptions, thereby improving agent performance in low-alignment environments without requiring base model retraining.**
